label: Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data

node.label: Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data
node.definition: Relational structures encode entities, interactions, molecules, networks, and non-Euclidean domains.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data is to connect Graph theory, Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors to downstream capabilities including Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Message passing & neighborhood aggregation, Graph neural networks.
examples: substrate for Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas
substrate for Message passing & neighborhood aggregation
limitations: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Graph theory, Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.

dependencies.rationale: Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.
Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
